parenting.definition: the job of raising and taking care of children
parenting.example: Parenting is one of the hardest things you will ever do.

legacy.definition: something passed down from the past — habits, beliefs, or values from your family
legacy.example: She wanted to leave a positive legacy for her children.

parent.definition: a mother or father; someone who raises a child
parent.example: Every parent wants the best for their child.

given.definition: received; what was handed to you (here: the kind of parent you had)
given.example: We cannot choose the family we are given.

children.example: Children learn by watching the adults around them.

say.definition: to speak words; to tell someone something
say.example: What you say matters less than what you do.

raising.definition: bringing up a child; taking care of them as they grow
raising.example: Raising a child takes a lot of energy.

escaping.definition: getting away from something you don't want
escaping.example: There is no escaping the rain today.

fact.definition: something that is true and can be proven
fact.example: It is a fact that sleep is important for health.

parented.definition: raised or brought up by a parent; how your parents treated you
parented.example: How you were parented affects how you raise your own kids.

influences.definition: changes or affects how someone thinks or acts

way.definition: a method or style of doing something
way.example: Everyone has their own way of dealing with stress.

significant.example: This is a significant moment in her life.

things.definition: objects, events, or ideas in general
things.example: There are many things to learn about being a parent.

shapes.definition: forms or influences who you become
shapes.example: Your early life shapes your personality.

people.definition: human beings; men, women, and children
people.example: Different people handle stress in different ways.

parents.example: Her parents taught her to be kind.

early.definition: happening near the beginning; in the first years of life
early.example: Early childhood is a time of fast learning.

experiences.definition: things that happen to you in life
experiences.example: Childhood experiences shape who we become.

lay.definition: to put down or create (here: your experiences create patterns in your brain)
lay.example: Good habits lay the foundation for success.

down.example: The teacher laid down clear rules for the class.

patterns.definition: ways of doing things that repeat again and again
patterns.example: She noticed patterns in her own behavior.

brain.definition: the organ inside your head that controls thinking, feeling, and memory
brain.example: Your brain stores memories from when you were very young.

expect.definition: to think something will happen; to believe something should happen
expect.example: Children expect their parents to keep them safe.

relationships.definition: connections between people — family, friends, partners

world.definition: everything around you; society and life in general
world.example: A child's view of the world starts at home.

yourself.definition: you, as a person (used to talk about your own identity)
yourself.example: Be kind to yourself when you make mistakes.

serve.definition: to help or be useful; to work well for you
serve.example: These old habits no longer serve you well.

trip.definition: to cause someone to fall or fail (here: 'trip you up' means cause problems)
trip.example: Old fears can trip you up when you least expect it.

especially.example: Children need love, especially when they misbehave.

become.definition: to start being something; to change into
become.example: She wants to become a teacher one day.

face.definition: to deal with something difficult; to meet a challenge
face.example: We all face hard moments as parents.

situations.definition: events or conditions you find yourself in
situations.example: Some situations make us feel uncomfortable.

unconsciously.definition: without knowing you are doing it; automatically
unconsciously.example: She unconsciously copied her mother's habits.

remind.definition: to make someone think of something from the past
remind.example: That song reminds me of my childhood.

childhood.definition: the time when you were a child
childhood.example: Happy childhood memories can last a lifetime.

good.definition: positive; something that makes you feel better or hopeful
good.example: The good news is that you can always learn.

news.definition: new information; something you are told
news.example: She shared the good news with her family.

prisoner.definition: someone who is trapped and cannot be free
prisoner.example: You don't have to be a prisoner of old habits.

past.definition: the time before now; things that already happened
past.example: Don't let the past control your future.

understanding.definition: knowing how something works or why it happens
understanding.example: Understanding your feelings is the first step to changing them.

inherited.definition: received from your parents or family — not just money, also habits and ways of thinking
inherited.example: She inherited her mother's love of cooking.

first.definition: before anything else; the beginning step
first.example: The first thing to do is take a deep breath.

step.definition: one action in a series; a single part of a process
step.example: Learning to listen is the first step.

toward.definition: in the direction of; moving closer to a goal
toward.example: She took small steps toward becoming a better listener.

choosing.definition: deciding which one to pick; making a decision
choosing.example: Choosing to stay calm is not easy but it is worth it.

keep.definition: to hold on to; to not let go of

change.definition: to make something different; to become different
change.example: It is never too late to change.

blaming.definition: saying someone else is the reason for a problem

becoming.definition: the process of starting to be something
becoming.example: Becoming a good parent takes time and practice.

conscious.definition: aware; knowing what you are doing and why
conscious.example: Make a conscious choice to listen more.

forces.definition: powers or pressures that cause things to happen
forces.example: Many invisible forces affect how we behave.

reactions.definition: the way you act or feel in response to something
reactions.example: Notice your reactions before acting on them.

respond.definition: to act after thinking about what is best — different from react
respond.example: She chose to respond calmly instead of shouting.

child.definition: a young person; your son or daughter
child.example: Every child needs to feel safe and loved.

rather.definition: instead of; used to show a better choice
rather.example: She chose to talk rather than shout.

simply.definition: just; only; without thinking about it much
simply.example: He simply walked away without saying anything.

react.definition: to act quickly without thinking, because of a feeling
react.example: Don't react in anger — take a breath first.

rupture.definition: a break or crack in a relationship — when something goes wrong between two people
rupture.example: A small rupture can be healed with an apology.

repair.definition: to fix something that was broken — here, fixing a relationship after a mistake
repair.example: Saying sorry is the first step of repair.

mistakes.example: Everyone makes mistakes — what matters is what you do next.

matters.example: What matters most is that you tried.

perfection.definition: being completely perfect with no mistakes — impossible for real people
perfection.example: Don't aim for perfection — aim for good enough.

afterward.example: She shouted, but afterward she felt bad about it.

acknowledge.definition: to say that something happened; to admit it openly
acknowledge.example: He acknowledged his mistake and said sorry.

work.definition: to put in effort to make something better
work.example: You have to work at a relationship to keep it strong.

shouting.definition: speaking very loudly, usually in anger
shouting.example: Shouting at children makes them feel scared.

shout.definition: to speak very loudly, usually because of strong emotion
shout.example: Try not to shout when you are angry.

mean.definition: to intend; to plan to do something
mean.example: I did not mean to hurt your feelings.

dismissing.definition: treating something as not important; ignoring someone's feelings
dismissing.example: Dismissing a child's fear makes it worse.

feelings.definition: emotions; what you feel inside (happy, sad, angry, scared)
feelings.example: It is important to talk about your feelings.

distracted.definition: not paying attention because something else has your focus
distracted.example: She was distracted by her phone and missed what her son said.

needed.example: He needed a hug after a hard day.

attention.definition: focus; giving your time and mind to someone or something
attention.example: Children need your full attention when they talk to you.

moments.definition: short periods of time; specific points in time
moments.example: Some moments stay in your memory forever.

inevitable.definition: sure to happen; cannot be avoided
inevitable.example: Mistakes are inevitable — everyone makes them.

means.example: Being a good friend means listening when someone is sad.

going.definition: moving; here, 'going back' means returning to someone
going.example: Going back to apologize shows real strength.

back.definition: returning to a person or place you were before
back.example: Go back and tell her you are sorry.

acknowledging.definition: admitting or recognizing that something happened
acknowledging.example: Acknowledging your feelings is the first step to healing.

happened.definition: took place; occurred in the past
happened.example: She explained what happened at school today.

saying.example: Saying sorry can be hard but it matters.

something.definition: a thing that is not named or not known exactly
something.example: I want to tell you something important.

wrong.definition: not right; a mistake; not the correct thing to do
wrong.example: It was wrong of me to yell at you.

feeling.definition: experiencing an emotion; having a sense of something inside
feeling.example: I was feeling tired after a long day.

frustrated.definition: upset because things are not working the way you want
frustrated.example: He felt frustrated when the computer crashed again.

took.definition: here, 'took it out on' means directed your bad feelings at someone else
took.example: She was stressed and took it out on her partner.

sorry.definition: feeling regret; wanting to apologize for something you did
sorry.example: I am sorry for what I said yesterday.

teaches.definition: shows or helps someone learn something
teaches.example: Life teaches us lessons we did not expect.

profound.definition: very deep and important; changes how you understand something
profound.example: That conversation had a profound effect on me.

withstand.definition: to be strong enough to survive something difficult
withstand.example: Their friendship could withstand any disagreement.

conflict.definition: a fight or disagreement between people
conflict.example: Some conflict in a family is normal.

end.definition: the finish; the point where something stops
end.example: A mistake is not the end of a relationship.

love.definition: a deep feeling of care and connection with someone
love.example: Children need to feel love from their parents.

take.definition: to accept and own (here: take responsibility means own your actions)
take.example: You should take responsibility for your words.

responsibility.definition: owning what you did — saying 'this was my action'
responsibility.example: Taking responsibility means not making excuses.

actions.example: Your actions affect the people around you.

capacity.definition: the ability to do something; having the skill or strength for it
capacity.example: She has a great capacity for kindness.

important.definition: having great value; something that matters
important.example: Sleep is important for your health.

avoid.definition: to stay away from; to prevent something from happening

cycle.definition: something that repeats in the same order; a loop
cycle.example: The cycle of arguing and making up kept repeating.

learn.definition: to gain knowledge or skill from experience

emotional.example: Emotional health is just as important as physical health.

resilience.definition: the ability to recover from hard times; to bounce back
resilience.example: Children build resilience through small challenges.

difficult.definition: hard; not easy to do or deal with
difficult.example: Having a difficult conversation is sometimes necessary.

survived.definition: lived through; got past something hard without being destroyed
survived.example: They survived a very difficult year together.

robust.definition: strong and able to handle difficulty
robust.example: A robust relationship can survive arguments.

enough.definition: sufficient; as much as is needed
enough.example: You are doing enough — don't be so hard on yourself.

handle.definition: to deal with; to manage a situation
handle.example: She learned to handle stress by taking walks.

disagreement.definition: when two people think differently about something; a difference of opinion
disagreement.example: A small disagreement doesn't have to become a big fight.

themselves.definition: the people we are talking about (used to emphasize 'they')
themselves.example: Children need to learn to solve problems themselves.

worthy.example: Every child is worthy of love and respect.

apology.definition: saying you are sorry; admitting you did something wrong
apology.example: A sincere apology can heal a broken relationship.

emotion.definition: a feeling like anger, fear, joy, or sadness

come.definition: to arrive or originate from a place or source
come.example: Where does this fear come from?

feel.definition: to experience an emotion or physical sensation
feel.example: It is OK to feel sad sometimes.

surge.definition: a sudden strong wave of feeling
surge.example: She felt a surge of anger when he lied.

anger.definition: a strong feeling of being upset or mad
anger.example: Anger is normal, but how you express it matters.

anxiety.definition: a feeling of worry and fear that something bad will happen

response.definition: a reply or reaction to something that happened
response.example: His response to the bad news was very calm.

done.example: What has your child done that upset you?

worth.example: It is worth pausing to think before you speak.

pausing.definition: stopping for a short time before continuing
pausing.example: Pausing before you respond can prevent arguments.

ask.definition: to put a question to someone; to request information
ask.example: Ask yourself why you feel this way.

reaction.definition: how you act or feel immediately after something happens
reaction.example: Her first reaction was to cry.

proportionate.definition: matching in size or strength — not too much, not too little
proportionate.example: Was your anger proportionate to what happened?

belong.definition: to be connected to; to be part of (here: the feeling belongs to the past)
belong.example: That fear doesn't belong to today — it belongs to your childhood.

older.definition: from an earlier time; further back in the past
older.example: Sometimes our reactions come from something older than we realize.

triggered.definition: set off a strong emotional reaction, usually because of a past experience
triggered.example: The loud noise triggered memories of her childhood.

mother.example: Her mother always told her to be brave.

named.example: A woman named Maria lives next door.

described.definition: told about something in detail; explained what happened
described.example: She described the event in great detail.

daughter.example: Her daughter loves to read before bed.

got.definition: became (here: got stuck means became unable to move)
got.example: The cat got stuck in the tree.

stuck.definition: unable to move or get out of a situation
stuck.example: I felt stuck and didn't know what to do next.

top.example: She climbed to the top of the hill.

jungle.definition: a thick tropical forest (here: 'jungle gym' is a climbing structure for children)
jungle.example: The playground has a big jungle gym.

gym.definition: here, 'jungle gym' is a metal or wooden structure children climb on at a playground
gym.example: The children played on the jungle gym at recess.

immediate.definition: happening right away; without any delay
immediate.example: His immediate reaction was to run.

flash.definition: a sudden, quick burst of something (here: a sudden feeling of anger)
flash.example: She felt a flash of fear when the lights went out.

danger.definition: a situation where someone could get hurt

perceived.definition: how she saw it; what she believed was happening (may not be the full truth)
perceived.example: He perceived the comment as an insult, but it wasn't meant that way.

helplessness.definition: the feeling of not being able to do anything; being stuck
helplessness.example: Helplessness is a scary feeling for a child.

wanted.definition: wished to; desired to do something
wanted.example: She wanted to help but didn't know how.

climb.definition: to go up using your hands and feet
climb.example: The child tried to climb the fence.

cannot.definition: is not able to; unable to do something
cannot.example: He cannot reach the top shelf.

get.definition: to move to a place; to arrive at (here: get down means move from high to low)
get.example: Can you get down from there safely?

reflected.definition: thought carefully about something that already happened
reflected.example: She reflected on why she had reacted so strongly.

later.definition: after some time; at a future point
later.example: Let's talk about this later when we are both calm.

realized.definition: suddenly understood something for the first time
realized.example: He realized he had been unfair.

punished.definition: given a negative consequence for doing something wrong
punished.example: She was punished for telling the truth, which was unfair.

patience.definition: the ability to wait calmly without getting angry
patience.example: Children need adults who have patience.

saw.definition: noticed; observed with your eyes or mind
saw.example: When she saw her child crying, she went to help.

fear.definition: a strong feeling of being scared or afraid
fear.example: Fear can stop you from trying new things.

frustration.definition: the feeling when things don't go the way you want
frustration.example: He felt frustration when he couldn't solve the problem.

felt.definition: experienced a feeling or emotion (past tense of feel)
felt.example: She felt happy when she heard the news.

little.definition: small; young (here: a little girl means a young girl)
little.example: When she was a little girl, she loved to draw.

girl.definition: a female child or young woman
girl.example: The girl smiled and waved at her friend.

dangerous.definition: likely to cause harm or injury; not safe
dangerous.example: Running near the road is dangerous.

week.example: She practiced every day for a week.

again.definition: one more time; happening another time
again.example: The baby fell down and got up again.

time.example: This time, she tried a different approach.

breath.definition: the air you take in and let out; 'took a breath' means paused to breathe and calm down
breath.example: Take a deep breath before you speak.

instead.definition: in place of; as an alternative
instead.example: Instead of yelling, she spoke quietly.

rescuing.definition: saving someone from a difficult or dangerous situation
rescuing.example: Rescuing your child every time stops them from learning.

scolding.definition: telling someone off angrily for doing something wrong
scolding.example: Scolding a child doesn't teach them — it just makes them afraid.

stood.definition: was standing; stayed in a standing position (past tense of stand)
stood.example: He stood at the door and waited.

nearby.example: She stood nearby in case her child needed help.

said.definition: spoke; told someone something (past tense of say)

see.example: I can see you are having a hard time.

think.definition: to use your mind; to consider possibilities
think.example: What do you think we should do?

try.definition: to make an effort; to attempt something
try.example: Just try your best — that's all you can do.

figured.definition: solved or worked something out on your own
figured.example: She figured out the answer by herself.

shift.definition: a change from one thing to another
shift.example: There was a shift in how she handled the situation.

dramatic.example: The change was not dramatic, but it was real.

outside.definition: from the view of other people; what can be seen externally
outside.example: From the outside, everything looked normal.

internally.definition: on the inside; in your mind and feelings, not visible from outside
internally.example: Internally, she felt proud even though she didn't show it.

broken.definition: stopped or ended something (here: broken a pattern means ended a repeating habit)
broken.example: She had broken the cycle of anger in her family.

pattern.definition: a repeated way of behaving or thinking
pattern.example: He noticed a pattern in how he reacted to stress.

passed.definition: given from one person or generation to the next
passed.example: These traditions were passed down from her grandmother.

generations.definition: groups of people born around the same time — grandparents, parents, children
generations.example: This habit has been passed down through generations.

noticing.definition: becoming aware of something; paying attention to it
noticing.example: Noticing your feelings is the first step to managing them.

belongs.definition: is connected to; comes from (here: the emotion belongs to the past)
belongs.example: This anger belongs to an old wound, not to today.

present.example: Try to stay in the present moment.

differently.definition: in another way; not the same as before
differently.example: This time, she chose to act differently.

require.definition: to need; to demand as necessary
require.example: Good relationships require effort from both sides.

curiosity.definition: wanting to learn and understand more; asking why
curiosity.example: Approach your feelings with curiosity, not judgment.

compassion.definition: caring about someone who is suffering; wanting to help them feel better
compassion.example: Have compassion for the child you once were.

once.definition: at a time in the past; formerly
once.example: Remember the child you once were.

comes.definition: arrives; when the time arrives (here: 'when it comes to' means regarding)
comes.example: When it comes to feelings, everyone is different.

part.definition: a section or piece of something larger
part.example: This is part one of the chapter.

chapter.definition: a main section of a book
chapter.example: Read the first chapter before our next class.

whether.definition: if; used when there are two possible choices
whether.example: It doesn't matter whether you win or lose.

might.definition: could possibly be; there is a chance
might.example: A rupture might be a small thing or a big thing.

did.definition: past tense of do; used to talk about completed actions
did.example: He did his best in a difficult situation.

like.example: Try saying something like 'I'm sorry I yelled.'

teaches.definition: shows or helps someone learn something
teaches.example: Life teaches us lessons we did not expect.

survive.definition: to keep living or existing through something hard

called.definition: had the name of; was known as
called.example: A feeling called anxiety is common in new parents.

place.example: Home should be a safe place for children.

well.definition: in a good or satisfactory way
well.example: These habits can serve you well in life.

ourselves.definition: us; used when 'we' are also the ones affected
ourselves.example: We need to be honest with ourselves.

ones.definition: used to refer to specific things already mentioned (here: which patterns)
ones.chinese: 那些（指前面提到的事物）
ones.example: Some habits help you; other ones hold you back.
